name: Ibero-Roman city of Castulo - circular via the Linares Greenway
name_original: Ciudad iberorromana de Cástulo - circular por la Via Verde de Linares
region_intro: Touring cycling around Linares offers diverse routes across varied terrain, suitable for different fitness levels. The region features both paved and unpaved surfaces, with options ranging from relaxed rides to challenging climbs. Cyclists can explore areas like the Sierra de Aracena, known for its scenic views and demanding routes, or discover cultural landmarks such as the Ibero-Roman city of Castulo. Best touring cycling routes around Linares The most popular touring cycling route is Ibero-Roman city of Castulo - circular via the Linares Greenway, a 15.9 miles (25.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours to complete. This route provides access to ancient historical sites via a dedicated greenway. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Plaza de Toros de Linares – Chimney of the Cruz loop from Antigua Estación de Almería-San José, a moderate 16.1 miles (26.0 km) path. This route passes by significant local landmarks. Local touring cyclists also love the Sanctuary of the Virgin of Linarejos – Chimney of the Cruz loop from Antigua Estación de Almería-San José, a 20.2 miles (32.5 km) trail leading through cultural points of interest, often completed in about 2 hours 50 minutes. Touring cycling around Linares is defined by varied terrain, cultural landmarks, and the scenic Sierra de Aracena. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more difficult routes with significant elevation changes. The routes in Linares are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 600 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Linares's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Linares?
A: Linares offers a wide selection of touring cycling routes, with over 50 options available. These routes cater to various preferences, from relaxed rides to more challenging adventures.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Linares?
A: The terrain around Linares is quite diverse. You'll find routes with both paved and unpaved surfaces. Areas like the Sierra de Aracena, associated with Linares de la Sierra, feature challenging climbs and scenic views, while other routes, such as those near Linares in Andalusia, are generally moderate and primarily on paved surfaces, suitable for all skill levels.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easy ride?
A: Yes, Linares offers several routes suitable for beginners or those seeking an easier ride. While many routes are moderate or difficult, there are at least 3 routes classified as easy. These typically feature less elevation gain and shorter distances, providing a comfortable cycling experience.

Q: What are some challenging touring cycling routes in the Linares region?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, the region offers numerous difficult routes, with 36 routes classified as such. These often involve significant elevation changes and can be found in areas like the Sierra de Aracena. An example of a longer route with significant elevation changes is a 104.8 km loop through Baeza, Garciez, and Jimena, as mentioned in regional research.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in Linares?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Linares are circular. Another example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38229534" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de Toros de Linares – Chimney of the Cruz loop from Antigua Estación de Almería-San José&lt;/a&gt;, which also forms a loop.

Q: What historical and cultural landmarks can I explore on a touring cycling trip in Linares?
A: Linares's touring cycling routes often pass by significant historical and cultural sites. You can cycle to the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996438432/ibero-roman-city-of-castulo-circular-via-the-linares-greenway" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ibero-Roman city of Castulo&lt;/a&gt; via a dedicated greenway. Other routes feature landmarks like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5302945" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Virgin of Linarejos&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38228656" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de Toros de Linares&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any natural viewpoints or scenic spots along the cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. The region boasts picturesque and diverse natural settings. You can find routes with stunning views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4008210" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rumblar Reservoir Dam&lt;/a&gt; and the surrounding wooded valleys. The Sierra de Aracena also provides a backdrop of scenic views for more challenging rides. Look for highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6698841" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Rumblar Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; for specific scenic spots.

Q: What do other cyclists say about touring cycling in Linares?
A: The touring cycling routes in Linares are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 20 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the opportunity to explore cultural landmarks, and the scenic beauty of areas like the Sierra de Aracena.

Q: Is it possible to combine cycling with visits to nearby lakes or reservoirs?
A: Yes, several routes offer opportunities to visit nearby lakes and reservoirs. For example, you can find routes that provide views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4008210" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rumblar Reservoir Dam&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5345028" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fernandina Dam&lt;/a&gt;. These natural features add a beautiful dimension to your cycling tour.

Q: What is the best season for touring cycling in Linares?
A: While specific seasonal data isn't provided, regions like Linares in Spain generally offer pleasant cycling conditions during spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). These seasons typically provide milder temperatures, making long rides more comfortable than the heat of summer or the cooler, potentially wetter winter months.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling options in Linares?
A: Yes, Linares offers routes suitable for families. The presence of moderate routes, primarily on paved surfaces, and loops under 2 hours with minimal elevation gain, makes them accessible for various skill levels, including families. Look for routes with lower difficulty grades for a more relaxed family outing.

A: While specific parking locations for each route are not detailed, many routes around Linares, particularly those starting from urban areas or well-known landmarks, are generally accessible with parking options nearby. Q: Can I find routes that pass by cafes or places to stop for refreshments?
A: Many routes in Linares pass through or near towns and villages, which typically offer cafes, restaurants, and other amenities for refreshments. tips:
The Linares Bullring, also known as the Santa Margarita Bullring, was built in 1866, and inaugurated a year later with a Miura bullfight, with the presence of the swordsmen Antonio Carmona, "El Gordito" and Manuel Carmona , "Baker".

   The most well-known and outstanding event that occurred in this arena is the death of the bullfighter Manolete in August 1947, gored by the bull Islero. Next to the gang gate there is a ceramic and marble plaque commemorating that tragic afternoon at the fair.

   It has a ring 50 meters in diameter and its alley 1.30 meters wide. It has a capacity of 10,500 spectators.

Spanish city and municipality belonging to the province of Jaén, in the autonomous community of Andalusia.
It is located in the central zone of the province of Jaén, between the eastern sector of the Sierra Morena and the upper part of the Guadalquivir river depression, for which reason two well-differentiated sectors can be seen: the mountains and the countryside.

It is an Ibero-Roman archaeological site a few kilometers from Linares.
In it you can see remains found in successive excavations that show the constructions of our ancestors.
